query_normalized: assessing the validity of statistical significance in randomized controlled trials with missing data statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) because it explicitly reviews Bayesian methods and multiple imputation techniques for handling missing data, which are the primary statistical mechanisms used to assess validity and perform inference in randomized controlled trials with missing data. Although it advocates for a Calibrated Bayesian framework rather than focusing solely on frequentist p-values, it directly addresses the core domain (missing data statistics) and the methodologica
